name: Peanut Butter-Chocolate Chip Poundcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease four 3 1/2-inch mini-loaf pans lightly.
3. In a bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat butter and peanut butter until pale and fluffy.
5. Add sugar and mix for about 30 seconds.
6. Beat in eggs one at a time until well combined.
7. Stir in buttermilk and vanilla extract.
8. Gradually add the flour mixture to the butter mixture on low speed, mixing just until blended.
9. Fold in chocolate-chip pieces.
10. Divide batter evenly among prepared pans.
11. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
12. Allow cakes to cool in pans for 5 minutes before transferring to wire racks.
13. Cool completely on racks before serving.
14. Store at room temperature for up to one week, or in the freezer for up to three months.
